item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You find it weird how every school and company does everything through trash software like Skype or Zoom? Well…

I mean, they should still use Discord, but I bet Foundation is memetically controlling them into avoiding Discord.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Googled that TOS and they really do have it! haha

item.title: No vote
item.content:encoded:
It's a cool concept, but it's a bit too corny for me. I'm not sure if switching Discord for a fictional app would have made it any better, but here are some parts that bothered me the most:

as Discord is known for its humorous loading screens.

To me this is too informal for an scp article. The article doesn't have to justify why a reader of the terms of service would think of it as a joke since anyone reading the article could understand why.

I started one of the bloodiest wars in history because I wasn't invited to a wedding.

I don't know a lot about Greek/Roman mythology so to me this is either: 1. A random assertion unrelated to any known myth, and so kind of pointless or 2. A reference to a myth, meaning that 5721-1 hasn't done anything better for the last 2000 years.

But then I remembered a tiny, tiny detail. I've read The Daily Crier. In fact, I read it pretty much every day. And I've never even seen the name Chelsea Reynolds pop up even once.

It seems kind of silly to me that the Foundation would send an operative with a half-assed disguise that can be proven false with publically available information. Given that they have to spread misinformation on a somewhat regular basis and even have an MTF dedicated to that it wouldn't be so surprising to find a few agents that have been working in a news organization.

Overall it's a cool concept, especially the idea that people nowadays wouldn't take a deal with the devil seriously even if they had already signed it. But it has quite a few things that bother me.

So I'm no voting

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm glad I'm not the only one who got that vibe from this article haha, it honestly reads a bit like Rick Riordan decided to write an SCP article in his free time (which is a good thing, it's very well written)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You want to give MORE eve to Gabe Newell. You mad(wo)man!!!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SOMBODY was a big fan of the Percy Jackson books
+1 from me

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The concept of an ancient forgotten god using modern tech to stay alive is okay, but there's room for improvement in this execution.

The interview log was hammy at best. Eris feels too much like a comic book villain and not a spurned god. The threat that ends the log is pretty good but the lead up is meh.

There is no reason the Foundation couldn't bring Discord down. Attacking the servers themselves to disable the service and prevent new users, using memetics, blah blah blah. The idea that they had to sneak someone in to interview Eris doesn't really work.

There were still good things. Like I said, Eris' threat was pretty good, the idea of her using technology to stay alive/charged up is pretty great, and Moines reaction to being told to kill herself was a nice touch and very in line with the Foundation.

-1 for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I do need to know merely one thing about it…

Light mode totally increases rate, doesn't it?

+1. Keep up the great work.
That's pretty clever, interesting, and well written on top of it. Nice job!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Huh. I always wondered where the name Discord comes from. +1

(Incidentally, gods like Eris, Acheron, Hypnos, Charon, Mnemosyne et cetera were never really prayed to. You have to search a long time before you'll even find a place where Hades was worshipped, and unlike Eris, he is a pretty important deity.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You had my full attention through the conprocs and the description, but I lost interest during the interview log.

When I read fictional gods, I want them to feel…I don't know, godlike. Alien. The dialogue should be weird and unsettling even if the god is benevolent, and especially if they aren't. It's not enough to be "cool and smart": I want something more, something inherently nonhuman.

The interview sounds more like TV villain dialogue from Buffy or Jessica Jones — and while I love both shows, it's more fun to hear "badass" dialogue rather than read it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Cernunnos means that the SCP in question can be contained, but due to logistical/ethical concerns, cannot. In the case of this SCP, it cannot be contained since you would need to kill off the entirety of Discord's userbase

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Gotta admit, I was very pleasantly surprised. A discord based anomaly initially sounded like a turn-off, but the execution was excellent. +1 , Great work mate

item.title: *closes Discord, backs away from laptop*
item.content:encoded: Ooooh, this was good

item.title: Cernunnos
item.content:encoded:
What does cernunnos mean?
I think you should add what it means

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Okay, seriously, as a Discord User, an Eris Worshipper (along with a couple other Hellenic Gods), and a "Foundation Member" this scip gives me some pretty mixed feelings, but over all, I fucking loved it. lol
Seriously, yes, this feels like something Eris would do, and I absolutely love it. Love seeing entities of my faith, part of why I loved 5997 as well, put into scips and taken seriously! Bravo! Wish I could give more than +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is dumb.
Had to log in to give it a +1.

It strains credulity a bit, and I believe there's already an anomalous religious TOS, but I'm a sucker for esoteric classes, and I've liked Cernunnos since reading Rituals. This really earns the containment class. Besides, what's a little EVE lost by Gamers™? Not like I'm using my vital energy anyway.

The actual interview was also really good. Someone else said it had too much fluff, but honestly fluff is what makes good writing.

Plus, you could tell me Discord really does have that in their TOS as a joke and I wouldn't know because I've never read it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
'Just let me kill off my uservase first'

This is the definition of a chaotic god IMO. I really enjoyed this piece as a whole, as I am a sucker for gods and god-like figures. +1 from me!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked the interview a lot, their interaction was fun to read. It's interesting how clever and prepared Foundation's agents are +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's a clever gag but the interview is ultimately too stuffed with filler, wrecking the pacing while leading to an unoriginal ending. The dialogue was snappily written but felt rather artificial and predictable. Not an irredeemable skip by any means, but I'm afraid it's a -1 from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Moines: You can't expect people to take that clause seriously. Gods aren't a staple of everyday life anymore.

This is not why you can't write stuff like "Also, you agree to pay us ten percent tithe of your wage for the rest of your life" into ToS: terms of service are not legally binding in any way. Surely an agent would have remarked on that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh hey, I remember reading a draft for this! The transcript has definitely improved, it's much more entertaining to see Moines play some cards first instead of Eris immediately shutting her down. Easy +1.

item.title: Re: this is it, the apocalypse
item.content:encoded: Glad to see this up! You put the work in and it shows.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's a reference to https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hunt_the_Wumpus, a classic text adventure game.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I liked the description better than I thought I would, but the interview felt too hammy for me. Novote.

Also I need to point out that the Foundation definitely would have the resources to end Discord. Introduce subtle memetic agents to get people to prefer other platforms, pumping millions of $ into Matrix (open source competitor), sabotage the product with security flaws that they then publicize to give bad PR, embed agents to weaken its EVE absorption, etc. You could argue Eris would cause the mass chaos it promised, but I don't feel the Foundation would sit around and let a diety control increasing a growing portion of society without a fight.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Probably some type of daimon, yeah. I'm gonna say it's a representation of Atë.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So does this make Wumpus a demigod? +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: steam voice chat's a thing

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvote, really excellent interview, to the point where I'm actually kind of mad at Eris, like… "She can't do this to the Foundation! This is an outrage!"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you! Eris/Discordia's characterization was something I did my best to get right in this draft. I wanted someone who could beat the Foundation not because they were a better chessmaster, but because they were entirely willing to throw over the table and hit you with the board, as I believe the mataphor goes. Glad you liked it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Then you'll just be channeling EVE to Scáthach instead.

Me, I've been dedicated to Goddess for like 25 years longer than Discord (the platform, not the concept) existed so…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked this a lot more than I expected to. Eris’ spiteful homicidal nature makes her a much more vibrant character than the opening suggests.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: … Honestly? Not enough to make me stop using discord if Skype's the next best option.

This was a really fun read, and I loved the mythological tie-in. I do think that the ending was a bit sudden, but that's less of an issue and more of a preference. The log felt realistic, almost like an actual interview transcript, and the characterization was very well done.

Also, I just had to make this after the GOC was tied into the anomaly.

+ Open Humorous Image
- Close Humorous Image

https://piccollage.com/_mfyMcjyM

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nothing's better than getting scared by an all-powerful madwoman. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A fun article. The interview log was a nice subversion of expectations and a good change of pace from how they typically go. +1

item.title: this is it, the apocalypse
item.content:encoded:
I'm waking up, I feel it in my bones
Enough to make my systems blow
Welcome to the new age, to the new age
Welcome to the new age, to the new age

Well, here we go. Six months ago, I had an idea about discord and goddesses. Today, finally got around to wrapping it up. Photo is from here.

Thanks to Roget, MalyceGraves, lt flops, Greyve, Jak, Barredowl, Roboticsquid, and Donkmaster, Lord of the Donks for critting it.

Check out my author page for more cool articles!
